reasoning: Without the Ukraine transit route (which ended in Jan 2025), Russia's physical capacity to deliver pipeline gas to the EU is limited to one string of TurkStream (~15.75 bcm/year). Adding Russian LNG exports to the EU (historically max ~20 bcm/year), the total maximum annual capacity is around 35-40 bcm/year. Achieving 15 bcm in a single quarter would require an annualized run rate of 60 bcm/year, which well exceeds the current operational infrastructure capacity directed to Europe. Since the market estimate is at 1% and the structural limitations strongly preclude hitting the 15 bcm quarterly threshold, the probability is extremely low. I am setting it at the lower bound allowed by the rules.
